sections.heading: What Is CNC Milling? Key Concepts and How It Works
sections.text: CNC milling is motion, metal, and math working in a computerized rhythm. You start with a block of raw material, feed the machine a few lines of code, and let it carve reality from coordinates. That’s the beauty of it: absolute precision, repeatable down to microns, yet flexible enough to shape anything from aerospace brackets to tiny medical implants. Most engineers know the end result, a perfect part, but not always the mechanics behind it. What actually happens between the model on your screen and the part cooling on the table? This guide breaks that gap wide open. We’ll dig into how CNC milling works, what makes 3-axis and 5-axis setups different, why metal behaves the way it does under...
